commentaries.author: Athanasius the Apostolic
commentaries.content:
he will send the One who was foretold to you, Jesus Christ:
And how can Christ be called a mere man? on the contrary, being made one with the Word, He is with reason called Christ and Son of God, the prophet having long since loudly and clearly ascribed the Father’s subsistence to Him; for when He had fulfilled His promise, He shewed, as was suitable, that He was He whom He said He had sent.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
The times of refreshment. The time of eternal rest and happiness
These words, you may be saved, must be understood, to make the sense complete. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content:
And then, when the time of consolation will have arrived.”:
Here he speaks of the Resurrection, obscurely. For those are indeed times of consolaton, which Paul also looked for, when he said, “We that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burthened.” (2 Cor. v. 4.)
